title: The Embodiment and Completion Ceremony
subtitle: A closing ritual for anchoring abundance as a permanent identity rather than a goal
excerpt: The Week 12 closing ceremony of the Money Journal program. Works through the five-part ceremony shape with the specific intention of releasing every trace of striving or scarcity and allowing abundance as a permanent, settled state — not something to reach, but something you are.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Week 12 Ritual in MONEY Journal (Rebecca Page, 2025). Used with permission as part of the homemade.education Mindset library.

practice.whenToUse: Use at a natural completion point — the end of a program, the end of a year, or the end of an intensive period of money work — to anchor what has shifted.

content.text: The closing ceremony of the 12-week MONEY Journal program. Designed to close a period of intensive money work and anchor the shift as permanent — from striving for abundance to being it. Works through the five-part ceremony shape: prepare, release, allow, integrate, anchor.
Sit quietly. Take several slow breaths. Picture a wide, clear space around you: your home, your financial situation, your life, all held in steady light. Nothing missing. Nothing to force.
I am ready to release every trace of striving, scarcity, and separation from abundance. I release it now. I release it now. I release it now.
I am ready to align to and allow abundance as my permanent, effortless state of being. I allow it now. I allow it now. I allow it now.

Take a slow breath in and all the way out. Let the sense of completion settle. The work is not finished — the identity is anchored. Abundance is not something you're moving toward. It is where you are.
Write this anchor statement in your own handwriting. Say it aloud:
Abundance is not something I reach for. It is who I am.
Adapted from the Week 12 closing ritual in MONEY Journal (Rebecca Page, 2025). The closing ceremony of the 12-week program.
